content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 13 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic)
**Hebrew Base:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), LC PDF pages 210–211 | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus, pa_0152
**Verse count:** 19 (following MT/LC numbering, v.1 = the summary verse)

---

**1** All the word that I am commanding you, you shall be careful to do; you shall not add to it and you shall not diminish from it.

**2** If there arises in your midst a prophet or a dreamer of dreams, and he gives you a sign or a wonder,

**3** and the sign or the wonder comes about of which he spoke to you, saying, "Let us go after other gods"—which you have not known—"and let us serve them,"

**4** you shall not listen to the words of that prophet or to that dreamer of dreams, for the LORD your God is testing you, to know whether you love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul.

**5** After the LORD your God you shall walk, and him you shall fear, and his commandments you shall keep, and to his voice you shall listen, and him you shall serve, and to him you shall cling.

**6** And that prophet or that dreamer of dreams shall be put to death, because he has spoken apostasy against the LORD your God—who brought you out from the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of slaves—to thrust you from the way in which the LORD your God commanded you to walk; and you shall burn out the evil from your midst.

**7** If your brother, the son of your mother, or your son or your daughter or the wife of your bosom or your companion who is as your own soul, should entice you secretly, saying, "Let us go and serve other gods"—which you have not known, you nor your fathers—

**8** some of the gods of the peoples who are around you, those near to you or those far from you, from one end of the land to the other end of the land—

**9** you shall not consent to him and you shall not listen to him, and your eye shall not take pity on him, and you shall not spare him, and you shall not conceal him.

**10** But you shall surely kill him; your hand shall be upon him first to put him to death, and the hand of all the people afterward.

**11** And you shall stone him with stones, and he shall die, because he sought to thrust you away from the LORD your God, who brought you out from the land of Egypt, from the house of slaves.

**12** And all Israel shall hear and shall fear, and shall not continue to do a thing like this evil in your midst.

**13** If you hear, in one of your cities which the LORD your God is giving you to dwell there, saying:

**14** "Worthless men have gone out from your midst and have driven away the inhabitants of their city, saying, 'Let us go and serve other gods,' which you have not known"—

**15** then you shall inquire and investigate and ask thoroughly; and behold, if the thing is established as true—this abomination was done in your midst—

**16** you shall surely strike the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword; devote it to destruction as cherem [devoted to destruction], and all that is in it and its livestock, with the edge of the sword.

**17** And all its spoil you shall gather into the midst of its open square, and you shall burn with fire the city and all its spoil, all of it, to the LORD your God; and it shall be a mound forever; it shall not be built again.

**18** And nothing of the devoted thing shall cling to your hand, so that the LORD may turn from the fierceness of his anger and give you compassion and be compassionate to you and multiply you, as he swore to your fathers—

**19** when you listen to the voice of the LORD your God, to keep all his commandments which I am commanding you today, to do what is right in the eyes of the LORD your God.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**Verse numbering (v.1):** All three agents note that the MT/LC places the summary command ("All the word that I am commanding you…") as the opening verse of ch. 13. Some English traditions assign it to 12:32. SOPHIA follows the LC numbering throughout Deuteronomy; verse count = 19.

**v.4 — "with all your soul":** EMET renders the Hebrew *nephesh* as "being," LOGOS and PNEUMA as "soul." SOPHIA adopts "soul" as the more idiomatic English rendering in the Shema-echo context, consistent with established Deuteronomy convention. See disagreements.

**v.6 — "apostasy" (sarah):** EMET and LOGOS preserve the literal force of *sarah* (rebellion/apostasy). PNEUMA paraphrases. SOPHIA retains "apostasy" as the precise theological term for this rare Hebrew noun. "House of slaves" is preferred to LOGOS's "house of slavery" (EMET's rendering is more literal and idiomatic).

**v.9 — "conceal him":** EMET "cover [for] him," LOGOS "conceal him," PNEUMA "cover for them." SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's "conceal him" as clearest.

**v.14 — "Worthless men":** All three agents agree on this rendering of *benei beliya'al* (lit. "sons of worthlessness"). EMET reads "Wicked men," LOGOS "Worthless men," PNEUMA "scoundrels." SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's "Worthless men" as the established convention (consistent with earlier books). See disagreements.

**v.16 — cherem:** SOPHIA preserves the Hebrew term *cherem* with a bracketed gloss [devoted to destruction] on first occurrence, following project convention for technical cultic vocabulary.

**v.17 — "a mound forever":** The Hebrew *tel 'olam* is rendered consistently across all three. SOPHIA retains this evocative phrase.

**LXX note:** The Vaticanus text (p. 152) aligns closely with the MT throughout. The LXX lacks "to your eyes" in v.18's closing clause (a minor minus). The LXX renders *sarah* as *apeplanesen* ("led astray") rather than "apostasy" — SOPHIA follows the MT.

*— SOPHIA*
